summary: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller soundboard captures a blistering 1978 show highlighted by a monster The Other One > Close Encounters > St. Stephen suite and an all‑time great Terrapin Station.
review: The recording is the definitive Charlie Miller SBD – clean, well‑balanced and free of audible glitches.  Musically the concert is consistently praised as one of the band’s peak performances, with a seamless second set that rolls through The Other One → Close Encounters → St. Stephen, a soaring Terrapin Station and an electric Jack Straw climax.  Reviewers note Jerry Garcia’s fiery lead work and vocal resurgence, making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 five‑star reviews uniformly praise both the sound quality (Charlie Miller SBD) and the musical performance; weighting 70 % show + 30 % recording yields a near‑perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Definitive Charlie Miller SBD – crisp, well‑mixed, no dropouts or muddiness reported; listeners repeatedly call it the best source for this show.

standout_songs: The Other One > Close Encounters > St. Stephen
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds high energy and fires up the crowd; second set is described as seamless and tightly sequenced, delivering a sustained apex through The Other One suite, Terrapin Station and an explosive Jack Straw finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead guitar is called ‘fiery’, ‘white‑hot Wolf tone’ and his vocals are noted as stronger than usual; multiple reviewers highlight his octave/divider work and improvisational brilliance.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum interlude within Terrapin Station is praised for being tight yet atmospheric, contributing to the set’s momentum.
